articles.title: Why petrol prices aren't rising as fast as expected despite halving of fuel excise discount
articles.description: Petrol prices are edging higher after the fuel excise discount was halved, but why haven't they jumped by the full amount?

articles.title: US futures rise and Asian shares trade mixed as oil prices decline with increased output
articles.description: Asian shares are mixed while U.S. futures are rising following a long weekend on Wall Street. Selling of technology shares pulled benchmarks in Tokyo and Seoul lower Monday. The U.S. dollar was trading at 162 yen. Oil prices slipped as some members of the OPEC plus group of crude exporters decided to raise their output next month. Uncertainty over supplies persists as negotiations with Iran aimed at fully reopening the Strait of Hormuz appeared to be on hold during funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Markets in the U.S. were closed on Friday, July 3, for the Independence Day holiday. This year, July 4th fell on a Saturday.

articles.title: Global Market: Japan's Topix rises on oil price drop, Nikkei weighed down by tech
articles.description: Japanese stocks saw a positive start to the week, with the Topix index extending its winning streak to six sessions, buoyed by falling oil prices and a strong global market. While the tech-heavy Nikkei 225 remained flat, sectors like shipping and autos surged. Investors are closely watching central bank policies and currency movements, with the yen weakening and government bonds experiencing a sharp decline.

articles.title: July 2026 car offers: Tata, Honda, Kia, MG, Hyundai, Maruti and other brands roll out discounts; check model-wise deals
articles.description: Car buyers can rejoice this July as major automakers like Tata Motors, Honda, Kia, and Maruti Suzuki are offering significant discounts. Savings can range from a few thousand rupees up to Rs 3.5 lakh on select models, including EVs and popular petrol/diesel variants. These attractive deals aim to boost sales following recent price increases by manufacturers.

articles.title: Hormuz Service Fee Explained: How Iran’s Plan Could Raise India’s Oil and Fuel Prices
articles.description: Will Iran’s proposed Hormuz service fee push up India’s oil import bill? Explore how potential charges on ships using the Strait of Hormuz could raise freight and insurance costs, impact crude supplies from Gulf producers, and affect petrol, diesel and LPG prices for Indian consumers.
